name: Donald Norcross
tweet: I remain in favor of the impeachment process. The future of our country is at stake. No one is above the law.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: Even before all of these actions took place or were documented, I voted to advance @RepAlGreen’s impeachment resolution in 2017 because I know that, as a co-equal branch of government, it is Congress' obligation to hold the executive branch accountable. We took an oath to act.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 🔟Trump's changing behavior toward Michael Cohen.  The Mueller report described how Trump’s conduct toward Cohen, a fmr Trump Org exec, changed from praise to castigation after Cohen began cooperating with prosecutors.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 9⃣Trump pressuring possible witnesses, including Flynn & Manafort.  Trump’s team asked fmr national security adviser Michael Flynn for a “heads up” on information & commended fmr campaign manager Paul Manafort for not “flipping."

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 8⃣Trump trying to get Sessions to take back control of the Mueller investigation.  The Mueller report says there’s evidence that one purpose of Trump asking Sessions to stop being recused between July-Dec 2017 was so Sessions would restrict the scope of the investigation.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 7⃣Trump trying to have Sessions curtail the Mueller investigation.  Trump told adviser Corey Lewandowski to have then-AG Sessions publicly announce the Mueller investigation was “very unfair” & have Sessions tell Mueller to limit future investigations in election meddling.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 6⃣Trump trying to stop then-Attorney General Jeff Sessions from recusing himself from the Russia investigation in the first place.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 5⃣Trump telling McGahn to deny that he had wanted Mueller removed.  Trump told then-WH counsel McGahn to “correct” a New York Times story that reported Trump had earlier instructed McGahn to fire special counsel Mueller.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 4⃣Trump attempting to stop the public from seeing evidence.  Trump directed aides not to publicly disclose the emails that set up the Trump Tower meeting (involving Russia providing information on Hillary Clinton) & Trump edited a related press statement for Donald Trump Jr.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 3⃣Trump trying to remove Special Counsel Mueller after he was appointed.  Trump reacted to news of Mueller’s appointment by telling advisers that it was “the end of his presidency.” Trump even called then-WH counsel McGahn & directed him to have Mueller removed. McGahn refused.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 2⃣The firing of FBI Director James Comey.  The Mueller report says “substantial evidence” indicates Trump’s decision to fire was the result of Comey's unwillingness to say publicly that Trump was not personally under investigation.

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: 1⃣Trump pressuring then-FBI Director James Comey to end the probe of then-national security adviser Michael Flynn.  Trump: “I hope you can see your way to letting this go.”

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: Do furloughed federal employees get back pay?  That is up to the House, Senate & White House. In 2013, furloughed employees received back-pay through the enactment of legislation.  ...I hope this information helps & that the #Trumpshutdown doesn’t last long.  11/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: What is the impact on food safety activities?  The FDA is unable to support the majority of its food safety activities.  10/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: What is the impact on medical research & the CDC?  NIH shuts down most medical research & the CDC has to stop flu season monitoring, as well as treatment & prevention recommendations.  9/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: What is the impact on federal housing loans?  The Federal Housing Administration stops approving applications for housing loans.  8/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: What is the impact on small businesses?  The shutdown halts federal loans to small businesses. 7/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: What is the impact on US military personnel?  The Defense Department says, “Military personnel on active duty, including reserve component personnel on federal active duty, will continue to report for duty and carry out assigned duties.”  6/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: What is the impact on veterans’ services?  All VA medical facilities & clinics remain fully operational. However, VA call centers & Veterans Benefits Admin public contact services are not available. 5/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: Will I still be able to visit the national parks and monuments?  During the 2013 shutdown, national parks, monuments & Smithsonian museums were closed. The Trump Admin said it will work to keep national parks “as accessible as possible.” 4/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: Will my mail still arrive?  Yes. The ~500,000 Postal Service employees are exempt from a furlough because the Postal Service is self-funded. 3/11

name: Donald Norcross
tweet: Will I continue to receive my Social Security & SSI checks?  Yes. You’ll continue to receive Social Security & SSI checks. However, new Social Security cards will not be issued. 2/11
